cover.title: Cloudless Q3 Review

cover.author: Finance & Platform

backCover.title: Thank you
backCover.text: Prepared by Finance & Platform with InstantCanvas. Questions to #q3-review.

blocks.text:
# The quarter in one page

Q3 closed **12% up quarter-over-quarter** with unit costs falling for the third consecutive quarter. The June launch of the self-serve tier pulled signups forward without denting conversion quality, and platform reliability held above target throughout.

- Revenue reached **$128k MRR**, ahead of the $120k plan.
- Gross margin improved to **81%** on reserved capacity and the storage migration.
- One SEV-2 in July (queue backlog, 41 minutes); no customer-facing data loss.

## Where the growth came from

Self-serve now contributes a third of new MRR. Enterprise renewals held at 97% logo retention, and expansion revenue covered the entirety of churn for the first time.

blocks.title: MRR vs plan
blocks.description: Monthly recurring revenue against the operating plan

blocks.text:
# Platform and cost

Infrastructure spend fell **8%** while traffic grew 19%. The two levers were the reserved-capacity purchase in April and moving cold artifacts to object storage in June.

## Cost detail

Compute remains the largest line, but the storage migration is the story of the quarter: artifact storage dropped from $9.4k to $3.1k a month at higher durability.

blocks.title: Spend by region
blocks.description: June infrastructure spend, grouped by cost center

blocks.text:
# Acquisition and funnel

The self-serve tier launched June 3rd. Signups accelerated immediately; activation held steady, which was the launch's main risk.

## Traffic composition

Organic remains the base of the funnel, but the launch-week content push shows in the referral band.

blocks.title: Weekly signups by channel
blocks.description: Stacked; the June launch is the inflection

blocks.title: June signup flow
blocks.description: Where June's 1,730 signups came from and went

blocks.text:
## Q4 focus

1. Ship usage-based billing for the self-serve tier.
2. Cut activation time below ten minutes with guided workspace setup.
3. Expand the EU footprint ahead of the two enterprise pilots signed in June.
